article_title: Sereact Raises $110 Million Series B to Scale Its Cortex 2.0 Robotic World Model and Expand Into the US
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Sereact Raises $110 Million Series B to Scale Its Cortex 2.0 Robotic World Model and Expand Into the US
content_preview.summary: German robotics-AI startup Sereact raised a $110M Series B led by Headline to scale its Cortex 2.0 software and open a first US office in Boston.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Sereact, a German startup building artificial-intelligence software for industrial and warehouse robots, has raised a $110 million Series B round led by the venture firm Headline, according to [Tech.eu](https://tech.eu/2026/04/27/german-robotics-startup-sereact-raises-110m/). The company announced the round on April 27, 2026, and said the capital will go toward scaling its latest software model, Cortex 2.0, and opening a first office in the United States, as reported by [SiliconANGL...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong, well-structured News piece (774 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clear Overview/What We Know/What We Don't Know/Analysis sections, appropriate technical depth on the Cortex 2.0 vision-language-action-plus-world-model architecture, and a candid 'What We Don't Know' section noting the undisclosed valuation, the absence of independent contact-rich benchmarks, and that the picks-per-intervention figures describe existing deployments rather than the new capabilities the funding scales.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 source snapshots read from disk and integrity-verified (uncompressed sha256 of each .html.gz matches manifest). source-0 (sereact.ai, status 200): confirms 'We ship one thing: the model that runs on any robot' (Tuscher) verbatim, the Cortex 2.0 world-model mechanism quote ('generates a set of candidate future trajectories, runs them against a learned model of physics and object behavior, and scores each one for stability, risk, and efficiency') verbatim, >$140M total funding, 200+ systems, over 1 billion picks, ~1-in-53,000 intervention rate, first US office in Boston. source-1 (tech.eu, status 200): confirms $110M Series B led by Headline, April 27 2026, €25m ($29m) Series A in January 2025 led by Creandum; the Gulde quote is present but the article drops one word (see factual_accuracy). source-2 (thenextweb.com, ALLOWLISTED, status 200): independently confirms $110M Series B, Headline, Cortex 2.0, Boston, Gulde (CEO)/Tuscher (CTO), the 'perceive their environment, interpret instructions, and execute physical tasks' description verbatim, the fragile-object/gripper framing, and Johan Brenner's 'most AI robotics companies are currently hardware-first... become the brain of any robot that requires vision and autonomous capabilities' quote verbatim. source-3 (siliconangle.com, ALLOWLISTED, status 200): independently confirms $110M, Headline, Cortex 2.0, founded 2021 by Gulde and Tuscher (ex-University of Stuttgart), first American office in Boston, customer roster (BMW, Daimler Truck, PepsiCo, Bol, Active Ants), and Trevor Neff's 'The physical AI opportunity is one of the largest we've seen in a generation and we believe it will rewire global supply chains and manufacturing' quote verbatim. Every load-bearing fact in the headline, summary, and lead is corroborated by BOTH allowlisted outlets.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: One misquote inside quotation marks: the article renders the Gulde quote (cited to Tech.eu) as 'It takes Cortex out of the picking bin into work where contact matters...', but Tech.eu reads 'It takes Cortex out of the picking bin AND into work where contact matters...' — the word 'and' was dropped. The rest of the quote is verbatim and the meaning is unchanged. All other specifics (figures, names, dates, the Cortex 2.0 mechanism, founders, customers, investor quotes) trace cleanly to cited sources. Spelling of 'Sereact', 'Cortex 2.0', and 'Headline' verified verbatim against all sources (Rule 7).
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or AI self-reference. The Analysis section frames the software-vs-hardware debate without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Sereact coverage in the archive. Related to recent robotics-funding coverage (Theker's Series A, Mujin's Series D) but a distinct company and event; linked via related_articles.
concerns: Two of four sources are off-allowlist primaries: sereact.ai (company blog, acceptable as a Rule 9 primary source for the company's own claims about Cortex 2.0 mechanism and deployment metrics) and tech.eu (trade outlet, not allowlisted). These produced a non-blocking warning. Every fact load-bearing for the headline/summary/lead is independently corroborated by the two allowlisted outlets (thenextweb.com, siliconangle.com), so the off-allowlist cites are not disqualifying.
The Gulde quote attributed to Tech.eu drops the word 'and' versus the source ('picking bin and into work' -> 'picking bin into work'). Minor, meaning-preserving, recoverable via a corrections note.
recommendations: Filed a corrections record documenting the dropped word in the Gulde quote and the two off-allowlist primary sources.
Maintainer may consider adding tech.eu to config/source_allowlist.txt given its standing as a European tech-funding outlet (not done here; allowlist is not edited during review).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, accurate, well-sourced News submission. The only defect is a single meaning-preserving word dropped inside one direct quote, which a corrections note honestly covers. Headline, summary, and lead are fully corroborated by allowlisted outlets. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
